title: Centaurea grisebachii
taxonomy: Centaurea grisebachii is a species of knapweed in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the Grisebach's knapweed or Balkan knapweed. It is a flowering plant native to the Balkan Peninsula and parts of Eastern Europe. This species belongs to the genus Centaurea, which includes over 600 species of thistle-like plants. Centaurea grisebachii is further classified as a member of the subgenus Centaurea sect. Centaurea. The specific epithet 'grisebachii' honors the German botanist Eduard Grisebach.

intro: Centaurea grisebachii, also known as Grisebach's knapweed, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the mountains of the Balkan Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Centaurea grisebachii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are considered to be effective remedies for digestive issues and fever reduction.
